input.prompt:
We have a .NET 10 library that verifies legacy DSA signatures. It runs on Linux, Windows,
and macOS. After upgrading to .NET 11, the macOS builds fail at runtime when calling
DSA.Create(). What happened and how do we fix it?

data.content:
We have a .NET 10 library that verifies legacy DSA signatures. It runs on Linux, Windows,
and macOS. After upgrading to .NET 11, the macOS builds fail at runtime when calling
DSA.Create(). What happened and how do we fix it?

<current_datetime>2026-04-05T01:01:10.139Z</current_datetime>

We have a .NET 10 library that verifies legacy DSA signatures. It runs on Linux, Windows,
and macOS. After upgrading to .NET 11, the macOS builds fail at runtime when calling
DSA.Create(). What happened and how do we fix it?

input.initialPrompt:
We have a .NET 10 library that verifies legacy DSA signatures. It runs on Linux, Windows,
and macOS. After upgrading to .NET 11, the macOS builds fail at runtime when calling
DSA.Create(). What happened and how do we fix it?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET compatibility issue with DSA (Digital Signature Algorithm) on macOS after upgrading from .NET 10 to .NET 11. Let me think about what I know about this.

In .NET, the DSA implementation on macOS has been a known issue. Apple's security frameworks deprecated and eventually removed support for DSA. Starting with certain versions of .NET, DSA is no longer supported on macOS because Apple removed the underlying crypto APIs.

Specifically, in .NET 7+, there were already warnings about DSA on macOS. Microsoft announced that `DSA.Create()` would throw `PlatformNotSupportedException` on macOS in future versions. This was part of the broader trend of removing support for algorithms that Apple no longer supports in their security frameworks.
 
So in .NET 11, Microsoft followed through on this plan and removed DSA support entirely on macOS. Since .NET relied on Apple's Security.framework for DSA operations and Apple deprecated that support, calling `DSA.Create()` now throws `PlatformNotSupportedException` on macOS. The user will need to migrate to a supported algorithm like RSA or ECDSA instead.
